scope_note: QA Witt Tower Ramified Prime e-VALUE RIGIDITY cert [436]. [435] generalized [434]'s p=5/Fibonacci-only closed form to 'any odd ramified prime p with e := ord(lambda0 mod p) as a free parameter,' verified on exactly two data points (e=4 for Fibonacci det=-1; e=2 for D12 det=+1). This cert shows that framing OVERCLAIMS: for the two-term companion-matrix families used throughout this chain (det=+1: M=[[t,-1],[1,0]], char poly x^2-tx+1, D=t^2-4; det=-1: M=[[t,1],[1,0]], char poly x^2-tx-1, D=t^2+4), e is NOT free -- it is mechanically forced by (det(M), which Vieta factor of D the prime divides): det=+1 gives e=1 if p|(t-2) and e=2 if p|(t+2) (only {1,2} ever occur); det=-1 gives e=4 for every odd ramified prime (only {4} ever occurs). [434]'s p=5 (e=4) and [435]'s p=3 (e=2) are not independent free-parameter samples but the only two possible outcomes of this law for their respective families. Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (ramification theory, double roots mod p); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (primitive roots, Hensel lifting); Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano-style period tables). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (C1) DET_PLUS1_E_RIGIDITY: for det=+1 family, every odd p|D satisfies e in {1,2} with e=1 iff p|(t-2) and e=2 iff p|(t+2) -- swept t=3..3000, 10691 pairs, zero violations PASS. (C2) DET_MINUS1_E_RIGIDITY: for det=-1 family, every odd p|D gives e=4 exactly -- swept t=1..3000, 6420 pairs, zero violations PASS. (C3) NO_THIRD_VALUE_GLOBAL: observed plus1 e-values = {1,2} exactly and minus1 e-values = {4} exactly across all 17111 pairs -- e=3,5,6,... never appear, directly falsifying 'free parameter' framing PASS. (C4) BRANCH_MECHANISM_EXACT: exact mod-p identities verified: det+1 p|(t-2) => lambda0===+1; det+1 p|(t+2) => lambda0===-1; det-1 => lambda0^2===-1 (forcing e=4 since -1!=1 for odd p) -- all 17111 pairs PASS. (C5) LEGACY_CONSISTENCY: [434]'s Fibonacci t=1 p=5 e=4 and [435]'s D12 t=4 p=3 e=2 reproduced via same generic code path, confirming refines rather than contradicts either prior result PASS. WHY NOT DUPLICATE: [434] and [435] treat e as an INPUT (computed once, plugged into period-set/multiplicity formulas); neither cert asks what values e can take. This cert is the first to characterize e itself, showing it is rigid. THEOREM NT: all arithmetic is pure integer mod p throughout; lambda0 and lambda0^2 are integer modular; no float, no observer projection. 5 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok (0.22s). Derived 2026-06-16.
